---
title: "Schedule at a glance"
subtitle: "STAT 45203 · Fall 2026 — a pacing plan"
toc: true
---

::: {.source-basis}
This is a **pacing plan**. **Blackboard is the authoritative source** for due dates, assignment links,
project checkpoints, and any changes announced during the semester.
:::

## Fifteen weeks

| Wk | Dates | Theme | What we build |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | Aug 24–28 | [Why assumption-light methods?](notes/week-01-why-assumption-light.qmd) | Models, assumptions, robustness, and "what is fragile here?" |
| 2 | Aug 31–Sep 4 | [Order statistics, ranks, ECDFs](notes/week-02-order-statistics-ranks-ecdf.qmd) | Quantiles, medians, ranks, empirical distributions, distribution-free summaries |
| 3 | Sep 7–11 | [Permutation logic](notes/week-03-permutation-logic.qmd) | *Sep 7 Labor Day; compressed W/F.* What changes under the null, what stays fixed |
| 4 | Sep 14–18 | [Randomization tests](notes/week-04-randomization-tests.qmd) | Design-based inference, reassignment, test statistics, *p*-values by simulation |
| 5 | Sep 21–25 | [Bootstrap distributions](notes/week-05-bootstrap-distributions.qmd) | Resampling with replacement, bootstrap statistics, sampling variability |
| 6 | Sep 28–Oct 2 | [Bootstrap confidence intervals](notes/week-06-bootstrap-confidence-intervals.qmd) | Percentile intervals, standard-error ideas, interpretation, failure cases |
| 7 | Oct 5–9 | [Rank-based one-sample & paired](notes/week-07-rank-based-one-sample-paired.qmd) | Sign tests, signed ranks, paired comparisons. **Midterm Fri Oct 9** |
| 8 | Oct 12–16 | [Two-sample rank methods](notes/week-08-two-sample-rank-methods.qmd) | Rank-sum logic, distributional comparisons, location shifts, interpretation |
| 9 | Oct 19–23 | [Categorical & ordinal outcomes](notes/week-09-categorical-ordinal-outcomes.qmd) | Ordinal responses, contingency tables, exact logic, scale-aware conclusions |
| 10 | Oct 26–30 | [Robust summaries & outliers](notes/week-10-robust-summaries-outliers.qmd) | Medians, trimmed means, MAD, resistant summaries, sensitivity checks |
| 11 | Nov 2–6 | [Robust regression ideas](notes/week-11-robust-regression-ideas.qmd) | Least-squares sensitivity, influential points, resistant fits, diagnostics |
| 12 | Nov 9–13 | [Comparing conclusions](notes/week-12-comparing-parametric-nonparametric.qmd) | When methods agree, when they diverge, and how to report the difference |
| 13 | Nov 16–20 | [Simulation study of method behavior](notes/week-13-simulation-study-method-behavior.qmd) | Type I error, power, bias, coverage, robustness, practical performance |
| — | Nov 22–28 | *Fall break* | No classes |
| 14 | Nov 30–Dec 4 | [Applied robust-methods report](notes/week-14-applied-robust-methods-report.qmd) | Project workshop: method choice, sensitivity, assumptions, communication |
| 15 | Dec 7 | [Final review](notes/week-15-final-review.qmd) | Last class meeting; synthesis and final support |
| — | Dec 9–15 | *Final-exam window* | Exact registrar block announced in Blackboard |

## Milestones

- **Midterm exam** — Friday, October 9, 2026 (in class). Covers the first-half material: empirical
  distributions, ranks, order statistics, permutation logic, randomization tests, bootstrap
  distributions and confidence intervals, and early rank-based methods.
- **Applied robust-methods project** — a final-third project: identify a data setting where assumptions
  matter, apply at least two reasonable methods, compare the conclusions, investigate sensitivity, and
  write a clear report on the tradeoffs. See [project guidance](resources/project-guidance.qmd).
- **Final exam** — cumulative; university-scheduled window December 9–15, 2026 (exact block posted in
  Blackboard).

## Important calendar dates

- Classes begin: **Mon, Aug 24, 2026**
- Labor Day (no class): **Mon, Sep 7, 2026**
- Midterm: **Fri, Oct 9, 2026**
- Last day to drop an individual full-term class: **Tue, Oct 20, 2026, 5 p.m.**
- Fall break (no classes): **Sun Nov 22 – Sat Nov 28, 2026**
- Last day of class: **Mon, Dec 7, 2026**
- Consultation day: **Tue, Dec 8, 2026**
- Final-exam window: **Wed Dec 9 – Tue Dec 15, 2026**
